name: First Commerce Bank
description_short: Community bank founded in 2002 offering checking, savings, business accounts, and lending products. Locally owned and managed with focus on personal service.
description_long:
First Commerce Bank was established on December 2, 2002, as the result of a local initiative by over 1,000 shareholders who identified a need for a locally owned and managed community bank. The bank was built on the principle of putting people first and maintaining strong ties to the communities it serves.

The bank offers a range of personal banking services including checking accounts for various customer needs, savings products, and mobile deposit functionality through their app. On the business side, First Commerce Bank provides checking and money market accounts designed for small business owners, along with multiple financing options including real estate loans, construction loans, and commercial loans. They also operate surcharge-free ATM networks and provide 24/7 debit and credit card support.

First Commerce Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused model and emphasis on local ownership and management. The bank actively transitioned customers from Peoples Bank of Middle TN, suggesting established relationships and regional presence. Their messaging emphasizes personal relationships and superior customer service as core differentiators, positioning themselves as an alternative to larger national banks.

As a regional community bank, First Commerce Bank serves both personal and small business customers in their local market. However, the website provides limited information about specific product features, rates, fees, or competitive advantages beyond their community-oriented positioning. Customers seeking detailed product comparisons or national reach may need to contact the bank directly for specific terms.
services: Lost or stolen card replacement support
pros: Locally owned and managed with over 1,000 local shareholders, providing community-focused banking
Mobile deposit available through their app for convenient check deposits
Surcharge-free ATM network access for customers
Specialized business products including money market accounts designed for small business owners
Multiple business lending options including real estate, construction, and commercial loans
24/7 debit and credit card support for lost or stolen cards
Products designed for different customer journey stages, from startup to established businesses
cons: Limited geographic reach as a regional community bank versus national alternatives
Website provides minimal information about specific rates, fees, or APYs for products
No mention of online-only account opening or fully digital banking capabilities
Limited details about customer service channels beyond card support phone line
No information about deposit insurance details, promotional rates, or account minimums
best_for: Small business owners seeking locally managed commercial banking and lending relationships
Community members in Middle Tennessee seeking personal banking from a local institution
Customers transitioning from Peoples Bank looking for account continuity
Businesses in early or growth stages needing customized financing solutions
diagnosis: First Commerce Bank is best for small business owners and community-minded individuals in Middle Tennessee seeking personalized service from a locally owned bank. The main caveat is the lack of transparent product details and rates on their website—potential customers will need to contact them directly for specific terms, fees, and competitive comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
